records.ety: [OE. love, luve, AS. lufe, lufu; akin to E. lief, believe, L. lubet, libet, it pleases, Skr. lubh to be lustful. See Lief.]

defs: A feeling of strong attachment induced by that which delights or commands admiration; preëminent kindness or devotion to another; affection; tenderness; as, the love of brothers and sisters.
quotes: Of all the dearest bonds we prove Thou countest sons' and mothers' love Most sacred, most Thine own.

defs: Especially, devoted attachment to, or tender or passionate affection for, one of the opposite sex.
quotes: He on his side Leaning half-raised, with looks of cordial love Hung over her enamored.

defs: Courtship; -- chiefly in the phrase to make love, i. e., to court, to woo, to solicit union in marriage.
quotes: Demetrius . . . Made love to Nedar's daughter, Helena, And won her soul.

defs: Affection; kind feeling; friendship; strong liking or desire; fondness; good will; -- opposed to hate; often with of and an object.
quotes: Smit with the love of sacred song.
The love of science faintly warmed his breast.

defs: Due gratitude and reverence to God.
quotes: Keep yourselves in the love of God.

defs: The object of affection; -- often employed in endearing address; as, he held his love in his arms; his greatest love was reading.
quotes: Open the temple gates unto my love.

defs: Cupid, the god of love; sometimes, Venus.
quotes: Such was his form as painters, when they show Their utmost art, on naked Lores bestow.
Therefore do nimble-pinioned doves draw Love.

defs: A climbing species of Clematis (Clematis Vitalba).
Nothing; no points scored on one side; -- used in counting score at tennis, etc.
quotes: He won the match by three sets to love.
A little western flower, Before milk-white, now purple with love's wound; And maidens call it love-in-idleness.
notes: ☞ Love is often used in the formation of compounds, in most of which the meaning is very obvious; as, love-cracked, love-darting, love-killing, love-linked, love-taught, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Loved (lvd); p. pr. & vb. n. Loving.]

defs: To have a feeling of love for; to regard with affection or good will; as, to love one's children and friends; to love one's country; to love one's God.
quotes: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind.
Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thy self.

defs: To regard with passionate and devoted affection, as that of one sex for the other.
To take delight or pleasure in; to have a strong liking or desire for, or interest in; to be pleased with; to like; as, to love books; to love adventures.
quotes: Wit, eloquence, and poetry. Arts which I loved.

defs: To have the feeling of love; to be in love.
